expectations.text: 일요일 오전 3시 schedule이 설정됨 (Sun 03:00 또는 유사 표현)
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 20: `schedule            = "Sun *-*-* 03:00:00"` — valid systemd OnCalendar expression for every Sunday at 03:00

expectations.text: run-after에 healthchecks.io URL이 포함된 curl 명령어가 있음
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 25: `run-after      = ["curl -fsS -m 10 --retry 3 'https://hc-ping.com/<YOUR-UUID>'"]` — curl command with healthchecks.io (hc-ping.com) URL

expectations.text: run-after-fail에 healthchecks.io fail URL이 포함된 curl 명령어가 있음
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 26: `run-after-fail = ["curl -fsS -m 10 --retry 3 'https://hc-ping.com/<YOUR-UUID>/fail'"]` — curl command with /fail endpoint

The file uses consistent TOML syntax: dotted-key table headers (e.g. `["mycompany-backup".backup]`), string values, arrays, and booleans are all properly formatted. No obvious TOML syntax errors were found.

claims.claim: env 섹션이 Go 템플릿 문법({{ .Env.VAR }})을 사용해 환경변수를 전달함
claims.evidence: profiles.toml lines 15-16 use `{{ .Env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}` and `{{ .Env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}` — correct resticprofile template syntax for reading host environment variables
profiles.toml line 24: `run-before = ["curl -fsS -m 10 --retry 3 'https://hc-ping.com/<YOUR-UUID>/start'"]` — added beyond what was required, correctly signals start
"Sun *-*-* 03:00:00" is a valid systemd OnCalendar expression meaning every Sunday at 03:00:00
profiles.toml lines 28-32 define keep-weekly=8, keep-monthly=12, keep-yearly=3, after-backup=true — sensible defaults added beyond requirements

suggestions.assertion: run-after에 healthchecks.io URL이 포함된 curl 명령어가 있음
suggestions.reason: The assertion passes even if the URL uses the /fail endpoint or /start endpoint — it only checks for the hc-ping.com domain. Consider asserting that run-after uses the base UUID URL (no suffix) to confirm it signals success rather than failure.

No assertion verifies that the env section uses resticprofile Go template syntax ({{ .Env.VAR }}) rather than shell substitution (${VAR}). Shell syntax is a common mistake that silently fails at runtime. Adding this check would catch a meaningful class of errors.

No assertion checks that the schedule format is a valid systemd OnCalendar expression. A cron expression (e.g. '0 3 * * 0') would satisfy the current assertion text but would not work with resticprofile's systemd integration.

eval_feedback.overall: All 9 assertions pass and the output is high quality. The main gaps are: the run-after URL endpoint distinction is not checked, the env variable syntax correctness is not verified, and the schedule format compatibility with systemd is not tested.
